records.ety: [F. vrifier, LL. verificare, from L. verus true + -ficare to make. See Very, and -fy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Verified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Verifying.]

defs: To prove to be true or correct; to establish the truth of; to confirm; to substantiate.
quotes: This is verified by a number of examples.
So shalt thou best fulfill, best verify. The prophets old, who sung thy endless reign.

defs: To confirm or establish the authenticity of by examination or competent evidence; to authenticate; as, to verify a written statement; to verify an account, a pleading, or the like.
quotes: To verify our title with their lives.

defs: To maintain; to affirm; to support.
